creator: rotkap@gmx.de
text:
this toppic seems to be old:


http://lists.helixcommunity.org/pipermail/video-issues/2004-December/000009.html

In my opinion:


a) the mplayer initializes the XVideo extension correct, but leaves it in a
"dirty" state

b) the vlc player does no initialisation (to work fine) but it leaves the
XVideo extension in a corrct state (means unchanged)


Finally - both players should be fixed:

a) the mplayer should set the state of XVideo extension into the
default state after finishing

b) the vlc player should initialize the XVideo extension correct -
maype another player crashed so an initialisation is needed
